snippets.title: Batching and slicing a stream
snippets.explanation: islice pulls up to n items at a time; the walrus loop stops when the chunk is empty. No full list is built.

snippets.title: Chaining and running totals
snippets.code:
from itertools import chain, accumulate

pages = [[1, 2], [3, 4], [5]]
flat = list(chain.from_iterable(pages))   # [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]

costs = [10, 5, 8, 3]
running = list(accumulate(costs))         # [10, 15, 23, 26]
print(flat, running)
snippets.explanation: chain.from_iterable flattens nested iterables; accumulate produces cumulative sums lazily.

snippets.title: Caching with lru_cache
snippets.explanation: Identical arguments return the memoized result, skipping recomputation.

snippets.title: partial and reduce
snippets.code:
from functools import partial, reduce

def call_model(prompt, *, model, temperature):
    return f"{model}@{temperature}: {prompt}"

ask = partial(call_model, model="gpt-4o", temperature=0)
print(ask("hi"))                    # gpt-4o@0: hi

total = reduce(lambda a, b: a + b, [10, 5, 8], 0)   # 23
print(total)
snippets.explanation: partial pre-binds keyword args into a simpler callable; reduce folds the list into a single value.
